question: 1. What is your age?
description: Let's start with some basic information about yourself. This helps us understand who we're serving.

question: 2. How do you identify your gender? Choose the option that feels right for you:
options: Transgender woman (assigned male at birth, identify as woman)
Transgender man (assigned female at birth, identify as man)
instruct: Choose the option that feels right for you:

question: 3. Who are you attracted to? (Your sexual orientation)
4. Where were you born? (Your native place/birthplace)
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu

question: 5.  Where do you currently live?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu

question: 5a.  What type of area do you live in?
6. Is your current place of residence the same as your birthplace?
options: Yes, I live where I was born (Skip to question 8)
No, I migrated/moved to a different place (Continue to question 7)
Prefer not to say (Skip to question 8)
description: These questions help us understand migration patterns within our community.

question: 7.   If you migrated/moved: When did you come to your current location?
7a. Did you migrate/move alone?
options: No, I moved with friends/community members

question: 7b. What was the main reason you migrated to your current location?
8. What is your religion?
9. Do you belong to any of these communities?
instruct: (This helps us understand if marginalized caste communities face additional challenges)

question: 10. What is the highest level of education you have completed?
11. What do you currently do for work/livelihood?
12. About how much money does your household earn each month?
instruct: (This includes income from all people you live with)

question: 13. Who do you currently live with?
14. Have you ever had sex with someone?
description: The next questions are about your relationships and sexual life. This information helps us provide better sexual health services. Remember, you can skip any question.

question: 14a. If yes, how old were you when you first had sex?
note: If NO, please skip to Section C on page _ (Violence section)

question: 15. In the last 6 months, about how many people have you had sex with?
16. What is your current relationship situation?
17. When you have anal sex, what role do you usually take?
18. Do you have oral sex?
19. In the last 6 months, how often did you or your partner(s) use condoms during anal sex?
options: Most of the time (75% or more)
I haven't had anal sex in the last 6 months

question: 20. Have you ever had group sex (sex with 3 or more people at the same time)?

description: Some people exchange sex for money or other things they need. There is no judgment - we just want to understand so we can provide appropriate support.
question: 21. Have you ever exchanged sex for money, food, a place to stay, or other things you needed?
options: Yes, I do this regularly now (within past month)
Yes, I did this in the past year
optionrule: If you answered 'No, never' - skip next 2 questions, go to Section C

description: Some people exchange sex for money or other things they need. There is no judgment - we just want to understand so we can provide appropriate support.
question: 21 a. About how many clients do you see in a typical week
22. In the past month, has any client forced you to have sex without a condom when you wanted to use one?
23. Someone has pushed, shoved, or grabbed me roughly
24. Someone has slapped, hit, kicked, or beat me
25. Someone has choked, strangled, or tried to drown me
26. Someone has attacked me with a weapon (knife, stick, acid, etc.)
27. Someone has burned or scarred me on purpose
28. Who hurt you in these ways? Select all that apply.
29. Someone touched me sexually when I didn't want them to
30. Someone made me touch them sexually when I didn't want to
31. Someone made me watch sexual acts or pornography against my will
32. Someone forced me to perform oral sex
33. Someone forced me to have anal or vaginal sex without a condom when I wanted to use one
34. Someone forced me to have anal sex
35. Someone forced me to have vaginal sex
36. Someone put objects or fingers inside me sexually without my consent
37. Someone forced me to participate in sex with multiple people
38. Someone blackmailed or threatened me to get sex
39. Who did this to you? Select all that apply.
skipLogic: If ALL Q29-Q38 = 'No' or 'Prefer not to say', SKIP Q39

question: 40. After this happened, did you go to a doctor or health facility?
41. Why didn't you seek medical care? Select all that apply.
options: I didn't think it was serious enough
I was afraid they would blame me
I didn't know where to go
I was afraid my family would find out
I was threatened not to tell anyone
Healthcare staff are discriminatory against LGBTQ+ people

question: 42. Someone has insulted, humiliated, or made fun of me because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
43. Someone has threatened to hurt me or people I care about
44. Someone has threatened to 'out' me (reveal my identity) against my will
45. Someone has isolated me from friends or family
46. Someone has controlled what I do, who I see, or where I go
47. Someone has destroyed my belongings or things that matter to me
48. Someone has used religious or cultural beliefs to make me feel ashamed of who I am
skipLogic: If ALL Q42-Q48 = 'No' or 'Prefer not to say', SKIP Q49

question: 49. Who did this to you? Select all that apply.
50. Someone has stopped me from working or earning money
51. Someone has taken my money or income without my permission
52. Someone has controlled my access to money or made all financial decisions for me
53. Someone has refused to give me money for basic needs (food, medicine, etc.)
54. Someone has denied me inheritance or property rights because of my identity
skipLogic: If ALL Q50-Q54 = 'No' or 'Prefer not to say', SKIP Q55

question: 55. Who did this to you? Select all that apply.

description: Sometimes people hurt us with words. These questions ask about verbal harassment and emotional abuse you may have experienced.
question: 56. How often does this kind of verbal abuse happen to you?
57.Do you use any dating apps or online platforms to meet sexual or romantic partners?
58. Have been harassed during the past year more than once while using the platform?
59. Did you report the cyber violence to the platform or authorities?
60. My family has verbally abused me because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
61. My family has physically hurt me because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
62. My family has forced me to marry someone I didn't want to marry
63. My family has forced me to undergo religious rituals or ceremonies to 'change' or 'cure' me
64. My family has forced me to see a doctor or therapist to 'change' or 'cure' me
65. My family has stopped paying for my education because of my identity
66. My family has threatened to disown or cut me off if I don't hide my identity
67. Have you ever been kicked out of your home or forced to leave by your family because of your gender identity or sexual orientation?
68. If you have experienced intimate partner violence, was the perpetrator:
69. How long have you been estranged from your family (if applicable)?
70. I have tried to change my name on legal documents (Aadhaar, passport, PAN card, etc.) to match my affirmed identity
71. When I tried to change my name, I faced difficulties, delays, or was denied
72. I have tried to change my gender marker on legal documents to match my affirmed gender
73. When I tried to change my gender marker, I faced difficulties, delays, or was denied
74. Because of mismatches between my legal documents and my appearance/identity, I avoid situations where I need to show ID
75. I have been questioned, harassed, or denied service because my ID doesn't match my appearance or affirmed identity
76. I was denied admission to a school, college, or educational program because of my gender identity or sexual orientation
77. I was forced to drop out of school or college because of discrimination, harassment, or lack of support related to my identity
78. I was denied use of bathrooms/facilities that match my gender identity in school or college
79. School or college authorities refused to use my affirmed name or correct pronouns
80. I have been denied government welfare benefits or subsidies (ration card, scholarships, pensions, etc.) because of my identity
81. I have been denied services at government offices (passport office, tehsildar office, municipality, etc.) due to discrimination
82. I have been mistreated, harassed, or asked to pay bribes by government officials because of my identity
83. I have faced difficulties accessing legal services or representation in courts due to my identity
84. I have been unable to open a bank account or faced difficulties with banking services due to ID issues or discrimination
85. I have been denied voting or faced difficulties voting because my ID doesn't match my appearance
86. I am unable to legally marry the person I want to because same-sex marriage is not recognized in India
87. Overall, I feel that laws and government policies in India do not protect my rights as an LGBTQ+ person
88. Have you ever used any of the following substances? (Check all that apply)
89. In the past 6 months, how frequently have you used any substances (other than alcohol or tobacco)?
90. Have you ever used drugs specifically to initiate, enhance, or prolong sexual activity? (Do not include alcohol, tobacco, or cannabis)
90a. What substances do you most commonly use for sex? (Check top 3)
91. Have you ever injected drugs (using a needle/syringe)?
91a. What substances have you injected? (Check all that apply)
92. Have you ever been tested for Hepatitis C?
93. Do you drink alcohol?
options: No (skip to Section E - Mental Health)

question: 93a. How often do you drink alcohol?
93b. On a typical day when you drink, how many drinks do you have?
93c. How often do you have 6 or more drinks on one occasion?

description: Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?
question: 94. Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
95. Not being able to stop or control worrying
96. Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
97. Little interest or pleasure in doing things
98. If yes, what diagnosis?
99. Are you currently taking any medications for mental health?
100. When facing problems, what do you typically do? (Check all that apply)
101. Have you ever accessed healthcare services (doctor, clinic, hospital)?
101a. When was your last healthcare visit?
101b. Where do you usually go for healthcare?
101c. Have you disclosed your sexual orientation or gender identity to healthcare providers?
101d. Do you have health insurance or any health coverage?
102. What are the main barriers to accessing healthcare? (Check all that apply)
103. Have you ever been tested for HIV?
103a. When was your most recent HIV test?
103b. What was the result of your most recent HIV test?
103c. [If Positive] Are you currently on antiretroviral treatment (ART)?
104. In the past 12 months, have you been diagnosed with any sexually transmitted infections (STIs)?
104a. [If Yes] Which STI(s)? (Check all that apply)
104b. Have you received treatment for the STI(s)?
105. Have you heard of PrEP (Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis - daily pills to prevent HIV)?
options: Yes, I understand what it is

question: 105a. Are you currently taking PrEP?
105b. Have you heard of PEP (Post-Exposure Prophylaxis - emergency pills after HIV exposure)?
options: Yes, I understand what it is

question: 105c. Have you ever used PEP after a potential HIV exposure?
105d. Have you received the HPV vaccine (prevents some cancers and genital warts)?
106. Are you currently using hormones for gender affirmation?
skipLogic: If 'Not applicable' or 'No, not interested', skip to Section G

question: 106a. [If Yes] Where do you obtain hormones?
condition: Show only if Q106 = 'Yes, prescribed by doctor' OR 'Yes, self-administering'

question: 106b. Have you undergone any gender-affirming surgeries?
106c. Do you receive regular medical monitoring while using hormones?
107. Does your family know about your sexual orientation or gender identity?
skipLogic: If 'No, none know', skip to Q108 (Social Support Networks)

question: 107a. [If Yes] How did they react when they found out?
107b. If you are married to an opposite-sex spouse, does your spouse know about your sexual orientation?
options: Not applicable (not married to opposite sex)

question: 108. How many close friends do you have that you can confide in?
109. How many of your close friends are LGBTQ+?

110a. [If Yes] How would you describe your relationship with your hijra community?
111. Where do you get most of your information about LGBTQ+ issues? (Check top 2)
112. Are you aware that Section 377 (law criminalizing homosexuality) was struck down by the Supreme Court in 2018?
113. Are you aware of the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019?
options: Yes, and I know what rights it provides

question: 114. Are you aware of any organizations or services that provide support to LGBTQ+ people?
114a. [If Yes] Which services are you aware of? (Check all that apply)
115. Have you ever used any of these support services?
115a. [If Yes] How helpful were these services?
116. After experiencing violence, have you sought help from anyone?
116a. [If yes] From whom did you seek help? (Check all that apply)
116b. Did you receive the help you needed?
116c. Have you ever reported violence to the police?
options: Yes, but they made it worse

question: 116d. What are the main reasons you did not seek help after violence? (Check all that apply)
options: Didn't think it was serious enough

question: 117. When you face problems or violence, where do you seek support? (Rank your top 3 sources)
options: I handle problems alone/don't seek help

question: 118. Have you ever contacted a helpline (phone/WhatsApp) when facing problems?
options: No, but I know helpline numbers
No, I don't know any helpline numbers

question: 119. Have you ever stayed at Garima Grih or any shelter/safe house?
options: No, but I know about it

question: 120. Have you ever received support from a Community-Based Organization (CBO)?
options: No, but I know about CBOs
No, I don't know what CBOs are

question: 121. In your hijra community, how often do you seek guidance from your guru for personal problems?
options: Always - my guru is my first support
Never - I don't seek guru's guidance
Not applicable - not part of hijra community

question: 122. Would you be interested in receiving training or capacity building support?
skipLogic: If 'No, not interested', skip to Closing Questions

question: 123. What type of training or capacity building would be most helpful to you? (Select up to 5)
options: Job skills training (computers, tailoring, beauty/salon, etc.)
Resume writing and job interview skills
Sexual health and HIV/STI prevention education
Mental health awareness and coping skills
Legal rights awareness (Section 377, Transgender Act, etc.)
How to file police complaints and access justice
Identity documentation (Aadhaar, voter ID, gender marker change)
Understanding and managing hormone therapy (for transgender persons)
Voice and communication training (for transgender persons)
How to respond to discrimination and violence

question: 124. What format of training would work best for you? (Select all that apply)
options: Training videos I can watch anytime

question: 125. What is the biggest barrier to your participating in training programs? (Select top 2)
options: Fear of discrimination at training venues
Fear of being outed if seen at LGBTQ+ programs
No training programs available in my area
Don't know where to find training opportunities

question: 126. Would you be willing to train others in the community if you received training yourself?
127. Have you ever received any training or capacity building from YRGCARE or other organizations?
options: Yes, from both YRGCARE and others

question: 128. [If Yes] How helpful was the training you received?
options: Very helpful - I use what I learned regularly
Somewhat helpful - I've used some of it
condition: Show only if Q127 = Yes (any option except 'No, never received training')

question: 129. What information or services would be most helpful to you? (Check top 3)
130. How would you prefer to receive support services? (Check all that apply)
131. Would you be interested in helping other LGBTQ+ people through peer education, support groups, or advocacy?
132. When you decided to pursue gender-affirming services (hormones, surgery, etc.), what did you hope to become or achieve?
options: To feel comfortable and authentic in my body
To be recognized and accepted in my true gender
To improve my mental health and wellbeing
To be able to have romantic relationships
To be accepted by my family
To live without fear of violence or discrimination
To match my appearance with how I feel inside
To be a role model for other transgender people
To have legal recognition of my gender
To simply be myself without hiding
Not applicable (not transgender or not pursuing gender-affirming services)

question: 133. How do you see yourself five years from now? What do you hope to achieve?
options: Being in a loving, committed relationship
Having children or raising a family
Being completely out and open about who I am
Having completed my gender transition (if applicable)
Living free from substance use issues
Having a strong network of friends
Being a leader in the LGBTQ+ community
Having learned new skills or trades
Having all my legal documents updated
I don't think about the future

question: 134. Who do you trust most to support you in achieving your life goals over the next five years?
options: Healthcare providers at LGBTQ-friendly clinics (like Sabrang)
No one - I don't have support

question: 135. What kind of support is most important for you to achieve your goals?
options: Help with job hunting or business
Just having someone who believes in me

question: 136. What should a much younger LGBTQ+ community member (18-25 years old) consider and plan for in their life?
options: Complete your education - it opens many doors
Get vocational training in a skill
Come out only when it's safe to do so
Don't rush gender transition - think carefully
Find and connect with LGBTQ+ community early
Be proud of who you are
Try to maintain family relationships if possible
Choose your friends and partners carefully
Don't depend completely on any one person
Take care of your mental health
If taking hormones, do it under medical supervision
Focus on building a career early
Don't rely on sex work as only option
Start saving money from young age
Get your legal documents in order
Don't be afraid to ask for help
Have dreams and work toward them
Help other LGBTQ+ people when you can
Life will have challenges - be prepared
I don't have advice for younger people

question: 137. Do you consider aging transgender individuals (60+ years) to be vulnerable or at higher risk?
options: Yes, definitely - they face many unique challenges
Yes, somewhat - they face some additional challenges
No more than anyone else aging
No, they are not particularly vulnerable

question: 138. What specific challenges do you think aging transgender and LGBTQ+ individuals face?
options: Lack of family support in old age
Lack of housing or being thrown out
Health problems and no access to care
Being unable to work or earn
Physical weakness making them targets for violence
No one to care for them when sick
Loss of dignity in old age
I don't think they face special challenges

question: 139. What should we do to support aging transgender and LGBTQ+ individuals?
options: Create old age homes specifically for LGBTQ+ elders
Provide regular pension or financial support
Create day care centers where elders can socialize
Legal aid for property and pension rights
Helpline specifically for elderly LGBTQ+ people
Intergenerational programs connecting youth and elders
Respect and dignity programs in healthcare
Training for healthcare workers on elderly LGBTQ+ care
Government recognition of LGBTQ+ elders as vulnerable group
Employment opportunities suitable for older adults

question: 140. Because of extreme weather, environmental problems, or climate-related difficulties, I have had trouble accessing condoms, sexual health services, HIV/STI testing, or staying safe during sex.
141. In the past 12 months, extreme heat has affected my daily life, work, or health.
142. In the past 12 months, flooding, heavy rains, or water-related disasters have affected me or my community.
143. Climate change or environmental problems have made it harder for me to access healthcare, medications, or health services.
144. Environmental or climate problems have increased stress, anxiety, or other mental health challenges for me.
145. I have had to move or migrate because of environmental problems, droughts, floods, or climate-related reasons.
146. During extreme weather or disasters, I faced discrimination or was denied help because of my gender identity or sexual orientation.
147. I worry about how climate change or environmental problems will affect my life and safety in the future.

options: A video call using an app such as Zoom or Google Meet
A text or chat-based platform (e.g., WhatsApp text, SMS, or online chat)
A dedicated health app on my phone

question: 151a. It would feel more private or confidential
